ogTitle: Alsace vs Alton Downs: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Alsace and Alton Downs.
heading: Alsace vs Alton Downs — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Alsace, Queensland and Alton Downs, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Alton Downs has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Alsace (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Alsace or Alton Downs safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Alsace has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Alton Downs scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Alsace scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Alton Downs scores 16/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Alsace or Alton Downs?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Alton Downs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
